articles.title: Nigel Farage Confidant Linked to $550K Loss On Iran Strike Polymarket Bet: Report
articles.summary: A close associate of Brexit leader Nigel Farage reportedly lost $550,000 betting on Polymarket that Iran would launch a major strike, presumably against Israel. The massive loss highlights how geopolitical prediction markets can attract high-stakes political insiders who may overestimate their access to privileged information.

articles.title: Polymarket Bets on Iran War Show Limits of Prediction Markets for Wall Street
articles.summary: Polymarket's Iran war betting markets are exposing flaws that make Wall Street nervous about adopting prediction markets for serious forecasting. The problem isn't accuracy but liquidity,thin markets with wild swings make it impossible for institutions to bet meaningful money without moving prices dramatically.

articles.title: The prediction market backlash has arrived
articles.summary: Critics are finally pushing back against the prediction market hype, questioning their accuracy and influence on everything from elections to policy decisions. The backlash threatens to slow mainstream adoption just as platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi were gaining serious institutional credibility and regulatory wins.

articles.title: Prediction Markets are a National Security Threat
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets pose risks to national security, probably because they could reveal classified information or be manipulated by foreign actors to influence public opinion. If regulators buy this argument, we could see new restrictions on what markets can operate and what questions they can ask, which would seriously limit the industry's growth.

articles.title: 2 young billionaires are behind the prediction market boom. They hate each other
articles.summary: The CEOs of America's two biggest prediction market platforms are publicly feuding, turning what should be a growing industry into a personal grudge match. This executive drama matters because when the heads of Kalshi and Polymarket are busy throwing punches instead of building better products, it gives traditional betting companies and regulators more ammunition to dismiss prediction markets as immature toys rather than serious financial instruments.

articles.title: Pa. lawmaker proposes regulating prediction markets in Pennsylvania
articles.summary: A Pennsylvania legislator wants to create regulatory framework for prediction markets in the state. This could open up another major US market for platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, potentially adding millions more users to the prediction economy.

articles.title: A Technology for a Low-Trust Society
articles.summary: Prediction markets are emerging as the go-to solution for navigating a world where traditional institutions have lost credibility and information sources can't be trusted. When people stop believing experts and media, they'll bet real money on outcomes, creating more reliable signals than any poll or pundit ever could.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Let You Bet on Anything, but This AI Stock Is the Surest Bet I've Seen
articles.summary: A prediction market bettor claims they've found the most reliable AI stock play available for wagering. The call matters because AI stocks have been driving massive betting volume across platforms, and if someone's cracked the code on consistent winners, other bettors will pile in fast.

articles.title: Polymarket Gets Major Prediction Completely Wrong After Loudly Boasting About It
articles.summary: Polymarket suffered a high-profile forecasting failure on a prediction they had publicly hyped as proof of their platform's accuracy and superiority. The embarrassing miss undermines the entire industry's credibility claims just as prediction markets are fighting for mainstream legitimacy and regulatory approval.

articles.title: Prediction markets are playing a dangerous game
articles.summary: Prediction markets are facing mounting regulatory scrutiny and potential legal challenges that could fundamentally change how they operate. The outcome will determine whether these platforms can maintain their current freewheeling approach or get forced into the same compliance straitjacket that's strangled traditional financial markets.

articles.title: Trump regulator splits GOP with prediction market fight
articles.summary: A Trump-appointed regulator is causing infighting within the Republican Party over how to handle prediction markets regulation. The GOP split could determine whether betting markets get the regulatory clarity they need to operate freely or face continued legal uncertainty that keeps major players sidelined.

articles.title: Bettors wagered $54 million on Khamenei’s death. Now they’re not getting paid.
articles.summary: Prediction market bettors placed $54 million in wagers on Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei dying, but the markets apparently aren't paying out despite his continued breathing. This mess highlights how even massive betting volumes can't force platforms to honor poorly structured death markets when the outcomes get legally or logistically messy.
